name: Lover
overview: Lover is a small village in the civil parish of Redlynch, about 1 mile south-east of Redlynch and roughly 7.5 miles south-east of Salisbury, within the New Forest National Park. Once called Warminster Green, it had become known as Lover by 1876, possibly a corruption of 'Lower Redlynch'. The village is built around a triangle of three roads - Besomer Drove, School Road and Church Hill - and has St Mary's church, built in 1837, with the adjoining Redlynch Village Hall nearby.
character: A scattered, wooded New Forest-edge village of mostly 19th- and 20th-century cottages and houses, quiet and rural, with the community-run Lover Green at its heart.
amenities: Redlynch Village Hall, near the church
Lover Green community green (former school field, home of the annual Lover Country Fayre)
The Kings Head at Redlynch and the Woodfalls Inn at Woodfalls nearby
No shop in the village - everyday shopping is in Downton or Salisbury

schools.name: Morgan's Vale and Woodfalls Church of England Primary School

commutes.notes: About 7.5 miles north-west for shops, services and the main-line station
From Salisbury on South Western Railway; fastest services about 1h 20m

prices.summary: Housing is mostly 19th- and 20th-century cottages and detached houses, in low volumes; the New Forest National Park setting and tight planning controls support premium, steady values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking, cycling and riding in the New Forest National Park on the doorstep
The annual Lover Country Fayre on Lover Green
Following the young River Blackwater, which rises near the village
pros: Inside the New Forest National Park with forest and heath access from the door
Characterful village with a famous name and a strong community
Good-rated primary school a short distance away in Redlynch
cons: No shop or pub within Lover itself
National Park planning policy limits new building and extensions
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quiet, characterful New Forest-edge village with countryside on the doorstep, who do not need shops or a pub in the village.

sources.label: Ofsted - Morgan's Vale and Woodfalls CE Primary (URN 140112, Good, 2021)
